evals.prompt:
Can you check this HTML for accessibility issues? It's a login form we're about to ship. ```html Login

Sign In

Log In
```
evals.expected_output: A structured accessibility report identifying: missing lang attribute, h1/h2 heading issue, inputs missing labels, button implemented as div (non-semantic), outline:none removing focus visibility, low contrast on button, error message not announced via aria-live, missing alt on logo image. Should include WCAG criterion references and concrete fixes. Should offer to apply fixes.

Report identifying: nav wrapper should be <nav> not <div>, logo img missing alt, nav items are <span> not <a href> (keyboard inaccessible, not semantic links), icon buttons are <span> not <button> and SVGs lack aria-label/title, active state uses only CSS class with no aria-current. Should reference relevant WCAG criteria and offer to fix.

evals.prompt:
I need to make sure this card component passes WCAG before we go live. We've been told it has contrast issues but I'm not sure what else is wrong. ```html
New

Wireless Headphones Pro

Premium sound quality with 30hr battery life

★★★★★ (128)
$89.99 $129.99
View details
``` ```css /* card.css */ .card { background: #f8f8f8; } .badge { background: #e33; color: #ff9; font-size: 11px; } .title { font-size: 16px; color: #555; } .desc { color: #999; font-size: 14px; } .count { color: #aaa; } ```
evals.expected_output: Report covering: alt text on product image is vague ('product' not descriptive), star rating conveys info via color/symbols only with no text alternative, tabindex=-1 removes button from tab order (critical keyboard access failure), multiple contrast failures (.desc #999 on #f8f8f8, .title #555 on #f8f8f8, .count #aaa on white, .badge yellow on red), original price #aaa likely fails. Should calculate or estimate contrast ratios, reference WCAG criteria, offer fixes.
